title: Enneastemon ochroleucus
intro: Enneastemon ochroleucus, commonly known as Ochroleucous Enneastemon or Yellow-green Enneastemon, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Enneastemon ochroleucus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects. Enneastemon ochroleucus is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments and support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Enneastemon ochroleucus is a species within the family Annonaceae, a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. This family includes over 2,100 species, which are mainly found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Members of the Annonaceae family are often evergreen trees or shrubs, and they can be found in a wide range of habitats, from forests to grasslands. Enneastemon ochroleucus, like other members of its family, is likely to be classified in the plant kingdom, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta. Further classification would depend on more specific characteristics and possibly molecular analysis.
